entry.title: SAP ATTP overview, Architecture and features
entry.content:
SAP ATTP – Overview, Features and Architecture

SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals (ATTP) is a specialized solution designed for pharmaceutical companies to effectively manage serial numbers, minimize compliance costs, and strengthen supply chain integrity. Its robust features ensure comprehensive visibility and traceability of pharmaceuticals, fully compliant with global serialization regulations.

SAP ATTP Overview

  • Ensuring Visibility and Traceability: SAP ATTP's primary objective is to ensure seamless visibility and traceability of pharmaceuticals by meticulously adhering to stringent global regulations on serialization, tracking, tracing, and reporting. This commitment fosters transparency, accountability, and trustworthiness throughout the entire supply chain ecosystem, ultimately enhancing the integrity and reliability of pharmaceutical products.
  • Overseeing Complex Supply Chains: ATTP plays a pivotal role in managing intricate supply chains, ensuring product genuineness, and mitigating risks associated with counterfeit activities or illicit diversions. This enhances brand reputation, bolsters consumer trust, and ensures stringent regulatory compliance.
  • Centralizing data and analytics: By centralizing product data serialization, tracking, and incorporating comprehensive reporting and analytics capabilities, SAP ATTP empowers organizations to make informed, data-driven decisions, enabling continuous improvement and operational excellence in pharmaceutical supply chain management.
  • Strengthening Pharmaceutical Supply Chains: Through fostering transparency, accountability, and operational excellence, SAP ATTP strengthens pharmaceutical supply chains, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, reducing counterfeiting risks, and building enduring consumer trust in the authenticity and safety of pharmaceutical products.
  • Risk Mitigation: SAP ATTP employs advanced algorithms and data analytics to detect and mitigate risks associated with counterfeit products and unauthorized diversions within the supply chain. By implementing secure serialization processes and authentication mechanisms, it helps pharmaceutical companies protect their brands and maintain consumer trust in product authenticity.
  • Equipped with robust features and functionalities, SAP ATTP serves as a vital tool for pharmaceutical companies to streamline serialization processes, mitigate compliance risks, and enhance supply chain security, ultimately contributing to the advancement of public health and safety.

SAP ATTP Features

  • Serialization Management: Serialization management involves the systematic assignment and tracking of unique identifiers (e.g., serial numbers) to individual units of pharmaceutical products. It ensures compliance with global regulatory mandates by enabling precise identification and traceability throughout the supply chain.
  • Aggregation and Disaggregation: Aggregation refers to grouping serialized units into larger entities (e.g., bundles, cases) for streamlined handling and distribution. Disaggregation involves breaking down these larger entities into individual serialized units. These processes optimize inventory management and logistics while maintaining traceability and compliance with serialization requirements.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Regulatory compliance in pharmaceutical supply chains entails adhering to stringent serialization, tracking, and reporting regulations enforced by agencies such as FDA and EMA. SAP ATTP facilitates compliance by providing robust tools for data capture, audit trails, and regulatory reporting, ensuring adherence to mandated standards.
  • Product Traceability: Product traceability involves tracking the complete lifecycle of pharmaceutical products, from manufacturing to distribution and beyond. SAP ATTP enhances traceability by capturing and managing serialized data at every stage, enabling rapid identification and resolution of quality issues and facilitating efficient recall management.
  • Reporting: SAP ATTP offers advanced reporting capabilities for generating comprehensive insights into serialized product data, compliance status, and supply chain performance metrics. These reports support informed decision-making and regulatory submissions by providing actionable analytics on operational efficiencies and compliance metrics.
  • Integration: Integration capabilities within SAP ATTP enable seamless connectivity with ERP, MES, and logistics systems, ensuring real-time data synchronization and visibility across the supply chain. This integration facilitates efficient management of serialized product data and enhances collaboration among stakeholders in compliance and operational workflows.

Architecture of SAP – ATTP

The architecture is designed to integrate seamlessly into pharmaceutical manufacturing and distribution environments, providing end-to-end visibility and control over product serialization, traceability, and compliance. Each layer plays a critical role in ensuring data integrity, regulatory compliance, operational efficiency, and secure data management throughout the product lifecycle.

  • Database Layer: Responsible for managing structured data storage, including serialization data (unique identifiers for products), transactional information (events related to product movements), and detailed product specifications. It ensures robust scalability to handle large volumes of data and maintains data integrity through transactional consistency and relational database management.
  • Application Layer: This layer hosts the core business logic and application services of SAP ATTP. It executes complex algorithms for transaction processing, manages serialization processes (assigning, capturing, and verifying unique identifiers), handles aggregation (combining serialized units into larger containers), and enforces compliance with regulatory standards (such as track-and-trace requirements).
  • Integration Layer: Facilitates interoperability between SAP ATTP and other enterprise systems such as ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning), WMS (Warehouse Management Systems), and external stakeholders' systems (regulatory authorities, trading partners). It supports various communication protocols (like web services, messaging queues) to ensure seamless data exchange, synchronization of serialization data, and real-time visibility across the supply chain network.
  • User Interface (UI) Layer: Provides multiple interfaces (web-based, desktop, and mobile) for users to interact with SAP ATTP. It offers functionalities for managing serialization activities (such as querying and updating product traceability data), monitoring compliance status, handling exceptions (like product recalls), and generating reports on serialization performance and regulatory adherence.
  • Security Layer: Implements robust security measures to safeguard sensitive serialization data and ensure regulatory compliance. This includes access control mechanisms (defining user roles and permissions), encryption of sensitive data during transmission and storage, authentication (verifying user identities), and authorization (granting access based on roles and permissions).
  • Analytics & Reporting Layer: Utilizes data analytics tools and reporting functionalities to generate actionable insights from serialization data. It provides predefined and customizable reports on supply chain performance, compliance metrics (like adherence to serialization regulations), and trends analysis. These insights help stakeholders make informed decisions, improve operational efficiencies, and ensure continuous compliance with regulatory requirements.

MAH View for SAP ATTP Architecture

The MAH (Marketing Authorization Holder) view in SAP ATTP serves as a specialized perspective within the system, focusing on enabling marketing authorization holders to manage serialization processes efficiently, ensure regulatory compliance, and gain actionable insights into supply chain operations.

MAH View encompasses several key functionalities and perspectives tailored to the needs of the marketing authorization holders in the pharmaceutical industry:

  • Authorization and Access: Customizes permissions and access levels specific to the roles of marketing authorization holders, ensuring appropriate visibility and control over serialization and traceability activities within the system.
  • Serialization Configuration: Empowers MAHs to configure serialization parameters such as serial number formats, aggregation rules, and other settings crucial for compliance with global regulatory standards. This capability enables MAHs to adapt to varying regulatory requirements across different markets efficiently.
  • Compliance Management: Supports MAHs in maintaining compliance with global serialization regulations through robust tools for serialization, traceability, and compliance reporting. This includes functionalities for capturing and managing serialization data, verifying product authenticity, and responding to regulatory queries or audits effectively.
  • Reporting and Analytics: Provides comprehensive reporting and analytics functionalities tailored to MAHs’ needs. This includes tracking serialization activities in real-time, generating compliance reports (such as serialization status reports and audit trails), and analysing supply chain performance to identify opportunities for optimization and regulatory adherence.

entry.title: Output management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Output management in S/4HANA

Introduction to output management

After working with many clients in different fields, I've noticed that most businesses struggle with output management. Each client has their own idea of what output means, whether it's PDF documents, forms, emails, or iDocs. While these views are somewhat correct, I think output management is about effectively communicating with customers or vendors using the right channels and documents.

When we talk about output management, I see it as combining "output" (like print, email, iDoc) with "management" (how we set it up to reuse the functionality). Output management in SAP S/4HANA is a big improvement in how businesses generate and distribute documents. This new framework solves the complexities and inefficiencies of the many old output solutions in previous SAP systems. This blog will mostly cover the output management features in on-premises S/4HANA.

Legacy Output Solutions

Historically, SAP provided several output management tools tailored to specific business areas:

  • SD Output Control (NAST): Used primarily for sales and distribution processes.
  • FI Correspondence: Handles financial document communications.
  • FI-CA Print Workbench: Manages print jobs for contract accounts receivable and payable.
  • CRM Post-Processing Framework (PPF): Deals with post-processing activities in customer relationship management.

While these tools were effective, they required customers to learn and maintain distinct frameworks for each business area. This led to a high total cost of ownership (TCO) and varied user experiences.

What is NAST

NAST is a central component in SAP for managing and controlling the output of documents. It is part of the SAP Output Control framework and is used primarily in the Sales and Distribution (SD) module, but its functionality extends to other areas such as Materials Management (MM) and Financial Accounting (FI).

Key features of NAST include:

  • Message control
  • Output type
  • Condition record
  • Processing routine
  • Transmission medium
  • Scheduling

NAST operates using the condition technique, which is the traditional method of working in SAP.

The Need for a Unified Solution

In SAP S/4HANA projects, the fragmentation of output management tools presented several challenges:

  • Complexity: Customers had to navigate and master different frameworks for different tasks.
  • Maintenance: Each framework required separate setup and ongoing maintenance.
  • User Experience: Disparate tools resulted in inconsistent user experiences across the SAP ecosystem.

What is Output Management in S/4HANA?

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA is a comprehensive framework designed to streamline the creation, formatting, and distribution of business documents. It modernizes and centralizes outdated output mechanisms, effectively meeting the diverse needs of various business processes.

Key features of S/4HANA Output Management include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Offers a single point of configuration for all output types, reducing complexity and easing maintenance. This common setup point can be used across different modules.
  • Flexibility and Scalability: Supports various output channels and formats, making it adaptable to diverse business needs and scalable for future growth.
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provides consistent and intuitive user interfaces, improving efficiency and satisfaction across different modules.
  • Simplified Maintenance: Eliminates the need for complex master data maintenance and extensive cutover activities.
  • Advanced Output Channels: Supports modern output channels like email, print, fax, and electronic data interchange (EDI), in addition to traditional methods.
  • Improved Template Management: Utilizes advanced tools for designing and managing document templates, such as Adobe Forms, enhancing document consistency and branding.

Different between NAST and Output management in S/4HAN

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

NAST is the traditional output control functionality used in earlier versions of SAP, such as SAP ECC. It manages the output of business documents through various channels like print, email, fax, and EDI. Key characteristics of NAST include:

  • Decentralized Configuration: Each application module (e.g., SD, MM, FI) has its own configuration, leading to complexity and redundancy.
  • Limited Flexibility: Customization and enhancement of output processes require significant effort and often involve custom ABAP development.
  • Condition Records: Utilizes condition records to determine when and how outputs are triggered.
  • Processing Routines: Depends on traditional forms like SAP script and Smart Forms for document formatting.
  • Separate Handling: Each business area (e.g., sales orders, invoices) has its own output determination and processing logic
  • It also covers Multiple Output Mediums and Error Handling and Monitoring

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

The new Output Management framework in SAP S/4HANA is designed to overcome the limitations of NAST and provide a more streamlined and efficient approach. Its key features include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Provides a unified setup for all output types, reducing complexity and maintenance efforts.
  • Enhanced Flexibility: Supports various output channels and formats with easier customization options.
  • Advanced Template Management: Uses Adobe Forms for designing and managing document templates, offering more sophisticated formatting and branding capabilities.
  • Output Parameter Determination: Utilizes a modern method for determining output parameters based on business context, simplifying the output determination process.
  • Improved User Experience: Consistent and user-friendly interfaces across different modules enhance operational efficiency.
  • Integration: Seamlessly integrates with other SAP S/4HANA components and external systems, improving overall document management and processing.

Key Differences

Feature

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

Configuration

Decentralized, specific to each module

Centralized, unified for all output types

Flexibility

Limited, requires custom ABAP development

Enhanced, easier customization

Forms and Templates

SAP script, Smart Forms

Adobe Forms

Output Parameter Determination

Condition records

Modern, context-based parameter determination

User Experience

Inconsistent across modules

Consistent and user-friendly

Integration

Separate handling for each business area

Seamless integration with S/4HANA components

Maintenance

Complex, higher total cost of ownership (TCO)

Simplified, lower TCO

 

Advantages and features of Output management

SAP S/4 HANA Output Management has the below features

  • Operates seamlessly in both cloud and on-premises environments.
  • Offers a unified solution for all output related tasks across the product and modules
    Reduce master data maintenance and relatively less time consuming for activation
  • Supports standard extensibility for configuration documents
  • Offers central monitoring tools for key users, enhancing oversight and control.

Based on Business Rule Framework (BRF), offering greater flexibility in managing output processes.

How it works?

For determining output types in transactions, we can utilize Output Parameter Determination (OPD). Maintenance of OPD can function similarly to master data or be set up via transport management. I recommend using OPD as a client-specific change. Using transports can lead to confusion, especially if other rollouts are occurring simultaneously.

Prerequisites –

There are few back-end changes you need to do it before maintenance of OPD setup.

  • Check if your application object type is active
  • Check if your Output type is available and if you want to use your own output then create new one by doing copy of standard output type.
  • Assign channel to your Output type.
  • Assign Form Template to Output Type
  • Assign Email template to output type.

To use Output management, we need to activate the required object and if we activate Output management then system will redirect output always to Output management and not to NAST.

 

Main parameters in OPD (Output Parameter Determination)

Below are the key parameters,

Output management is BRF based functionality which can be setup by using below 7 steps in the SAP using OPD.

Let’s check in the system –

OPD (Output parameter determination) screen

There are multiple steps (as per above screenshot) which should be maintained to determine output in billing document as per this example, here we will take billing output type as an example.

1. Output Type –

This step begins by deciding whether to process the output immediately or later via a job. As shown below, for the combination of billing type F2 and output type BILLING_DOCUMENT, we can choose to process the output type immediately or through a background job (VF31F) at the time of billing document creation. Additionally, in the billing document, we can manually add the output type and set the dispatch time manually.

2. Receiver –

In this step, we can determine the output type for a specific role (partner function). If we have four lines with different roles and want to consider only the first two, we will mark the Exclusive indicator 'X' for the second line. This way, the system will only consider the first two entries and ignore any entries after the second line.

3. Channel –

In this stage, the 'Channel' will be defined. The channel could be IDoc, Print, Email, etc. This stage will also include an exclusive indicator.

For instance, if the exclusive indicator 'X' is set for IDoc as shown below, the system will not search for or determine the PRINT and EMAIL channels.

4. Printer setting –

In this phase, we can specify the printer and the number of copies to be printed after creating the document. Before setting up the printer in this phase, it is necessary to install the printer in SAP.

Additionally, please be aware that the new output management system supports Printer Spool Devices limited to PDF device types only (PDFUC: PDF Unicode 1.3 / PDF1: PDF ISO Latin1 4.60+).

This restriction is detailed under the Symptoms section in note 268480 and SAP Note. 2294198

5. Email setting –

In this phase, the sender's email ID can be configured. This means that when an email is sent to the customer, the sender's email will be set up here, and the recipient's email will be the customer's email address.

Additionally, we can also establish the email template in this phase. An email template specifies the format and content that the customer will receive from us via email.

6. Email recipient –

The email address (recipient address) retrieved in the billing document is sourced from BP master data or determined through this step.

If you have configured an email address here, this setting takes precedence.

Multiple email addresses can be maintained in this determination step by separating them with ';'.
You can also define the email type code with TO, CC, BCC.

7. Form Template –

This step is intended for maintaining the form template used for each output. Ensure that the form template maintained here has been assigned to the output type in SPRO -> Cross-Application Components -> Output Control -> Assign Form Templates.

If the language is not specified in OPD, it will be retrieved from the customer master data of the output recipient.

8. Output Relevance –

This step verifies if the output item is flagged as relevant for output.

If the relevance indicator is not set to 'X - true', the output item determined in the billing document will not be considered relevant for output.

Consequently, its status will remain 'In preparation' in the billing document under the output section.

OPD functionalities –

Adding extra predefined standard fields

Adding new columns or fields: OPD provides default fields that cover all necessary steps we've discussed. However, often after client discussions, we realize additional parameters are needed to define our objects in OPD differently.

In OPD, there are additional fields available that we can add as columns on the main OPD screen. These can then be utilized in determination combinations as needed.

To do this, navigate to Edit -> Table settings -> Insert column -> From context data object -> Search.

Click on search

Following this step, a list of standard additional fields will be available for us to add and utilize in OPD. If the required field is still not available, we can proceed with an enhancement to add the necessary field to this list. This process requires the involvement of an ABAP specialist.

Simulation functionality

Having reviewed all seven steps, we can use them to determine the output type in the transaction.

To preview the output in simulation mode, use the highlighted option below. This allows us to simulate the output effectively.

Key points and learnings –

Pros

  • As OPD is BRF based functionality, we can import and export data using predefined template.
  • New column (fields) can be added and can be removed as per requirement.
  • SAP S/4HANA output management is recommended but not mandatory.
  • The scope of SAP S/4HANA output management is not comparable with the scope of NAST.
  • SAP S/4HANA output control is not designed to completely replace NAST.
  • Innovations will only be made in the new framework (SAP S/4HANA output management)

Cons –

  • It will only cover the output part of NAST (print, email, partly EDI).
  • The rest – like system integration, workflow, or special function – is not (and will not be) in the scope of SAP S/4HANA output control.
  • Customers will lose all NAST features when switching to SAP S/4HANA Output Control. Using S/4HANA Output Control for applications that previously relied on NAST is only advisable in exceptional cases
  • iDocs are not fully supported by the new output management. There are few restrictions as compared to NAST.
  • NO ALE is supported in Output management.
  • No tool is available for conversion from NAST to Output management.

Reference –

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/output-management-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13492419

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/d7bd4b5d70d94f09bcf99b221a7a1688.html

Check out SAP notes related to the topic -

  • SAP Note 2292539 - SAP S/4HANA output control – configuration
  • 2843926 - It is not possible to activate rules in App 'Output Parameter Determination' - SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Note 2228611 –Output Management in S/4HANA 
  • SAP Note 2248229 –SAP S/4HANA output control - download XML file
  • SAP Note 2292571 –SAP S/4HANA output control -technical setup 
  • SAP Note 2292646 –SAP S/4HANA output control -form templates with fragments 
  • SAP Note 2292681 –SAP S/4HANA output control –master form templates 
  • SAP Note 2294198 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized forms 
  • SAP Note 2367080 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized master forms

entry.title: Fiscal Year Variant
entry.content:
Fiscal Year Variant:

Defines Financial Year, Defines how many normal periods and special periods for the financial year.

Normal Financial year contains 12 normal periods plus up to 4 Special Periods

Normal periods always start from period1 to 12

Normal periods contains the period definitions.

Special periods does not contains the period definitions

Period definition :

It defines each period start date and end date of the period

Example

Period1--->from April1 to April 30th

Period2 ----> From 1st May to 31st May

Period12-----> From 1st March to 31st March

Type of the Fiscal Year:

1.Calendar Fiscal Year

2.Non Calendar Fiscal Year

1.Calendar Fiscal Year:

Those Financial Year Posting Period should be equal to Calendar Month.

Example: Period1 = Jan , Period2 = Feb , Period3 = Mar , Period4 = Apr , Period5 = May , Period6 = June , Period7 =July Period12 = Dec

Every Period Start Date and End Date should be equal to Calendar Month that type of Financial Year can be called as Calendar Year.

Period1----> 1st jan to 31st jan

Period2----> 1st Feb to 28th feb or 29th Feb

Period3 ----< 1St March to 31st March

 

Period12----> 1st Dec to 31st Dec

Note :

Not required to define any period definition

System follows standard functionality if FY defined / activated with Calendar year indicator

In the Calendar Fiscal Year, the Posting Periods and Fiscal Year Determines based on Posting Dates

Posting date : 20.03.2022======> system determines Posting Period =3 and Fiscal Year =2022

Not required any mapping rules for period definitions in case of FY as a calendar year

2.Non Calendar Fiscal Year:

Other than Jan to Dec FY can be called as Non Calendar year

Example :  April to March

                 July to June

                 Oct to Sept

Even though FY follows Jan to dec but period definition not equal to calendar month start date and end date

FY====> 1st Jan to 31st Dec

Period 1------> 1st Jan to 29th Jan             not equal to calendar Month # Jan

Period2-------> 30th Jan to 28th Feb          not equal to calendar Month # Feb

Period3-------> 29th Feb to 30th March      not equal to calendar Month # March

Note :

Posting date : 20.03.2022====> How the system knows what is the posting period = ???? What is the Fiscal year = ???

in case of non calendar FY , Need to specify the period definitions to get the Posting period and FY

In case of Non-Calendar Year ,the Posting Period and Fiscal Year determine based on posting date along with Fiscal Year Variant Configuration Settings

Definition :

In Case of Non-Calendar Year, we need to maintain relationship between Calendar Month and Calendar Day and Posting Period

Year Shift Indicator:

in case of Calendar FY====> Not applicable year shift indicator

in case of Non Calendar FY====> applicable / required year shift indicator

Calendar FY 

 Example 2021----> Period1 (01.01.2021 to 31.01.2021) …….. Period12 (01.12.2021 to 31.12.2021)

in the above case , FY Periods and calendar FY are identical

Calendar Year ===>2021=====> FY 2021===> Periods and calendar months are identical

In the above case , not required year shift indicator functionality

Non calendar FY

Few periods belong to One calendar year and few periods belongs to other calendar year

Example : FY 2021 Apr to March

Example : Define FY Oct to Sept

Need to use year shift indicator to bring the different calendar year periods under one Fiscal year for non calendar FY.

Maintain Fiscal Year Variant:

SPRO -> SAP Reference IMG -> Financial Accounting -> Financial Accounting Global Settings -> Ledgers -> Fiscal Year and Posting Periods -> Maintain Fiscal Year Variant (OB29)

Maintaining standard FV for Company Code

Assign Company code to a Fiscal Variant:

SPRO -> SAP Reference IMG -> Financial Accounting -> Financial Accounting Global Settings -> Ledgers -> Fiscal Year and Posting Periods -> Assign Company Code to a Fiscal Variant (OB37)

Note: Creating our own Fiscal variants

Z1 --> FY var for Calendar Year -->  Jan to dec

Z2 --> FY Apr to March

Period Text=====> Its optional

==> Possible to define our own text both in English , Local languages

==> It follows the standard text if not maintained your own text

 

Year Independent FY Variant:

Can be defined either calendar year or Non calendar year

FY period definition is constant irrespective of the Fiscal year

Period definition it wouldn't vary depends on the FY

Example :

FY===> Apr to march

FY===> Jan to Dec

FY===> July to June

FY===> Oct to Sept

Note : In the above case , the above period definition not specified any specific to FY===> these type of FY can be called as year Independent FY

Year dependent FY Variant:

Possible to define only Non calendar years

FY period definition is not constant and period definition it may vary depends on the Fiscal year

Period definition it may vary depends on the FY

Example1 :Define FY Jan to Dec and every period should end with last working day of the calendar month only Sunday is Holiday.

Short End Fiscal Year:

Those fiscal year posting periods less than the 12 calendar months can be called as shortened fiscal year.

Fiscal year Conversions ( Specific year conversion)

      Business Use New case :

            Newly incorporation of a company or newly starting a business during the year.

            Winding up and merging of the company during the year, the specific year becomes the shortened              FY

    Shifting from Calendar year to Non Calendar Year or Vice versa.

            FY Jan to dec====> FY Oct to Sept

            FY Jan to dec====> FY APR to March

   Shifting from Non Calendar year to Calendar Year

          FY Oct to Sept====> FY Jan to Dec

         FY Apr to March ====> FY Jan to Dec 

Business Example :

Business need to shift from Jan to dec into APR to March

FY2020Jan 2020 to Dec 2020   ======> Full Financial YearYear dependent FY
FY2021Jan 2021 to  March 2021  ======> Shortened  FYYear dependent FY
FY2022APR 2021  to March2022   ======> Full Financial YearYear dependent FY
FY2023APR 2022  to March2023   ======> Full Financial YearYear dependent FY

Process steps to be followed

1.Need to complete all the period end activities

   Month end closing process to be completed

   FI Period end process

   CO period end process

2.Apply the FY conversions

   Use the Same FY variant code ( example Y8)

   Need to activate Year dependent FY indicator , Short FY indicator for FY variant Y8 for the year 2021

   Need to define Year dependent FY variant for year 2021

   Need to define Year dependent FY for future atleast to 10 years to 20years ( like 2022, 2023, 2024 till 2041)

   Need to define Year dependent FY for past atleast 10years to 20 years back ( like 2019,2018,2017,2016,2015 -------->1990)

3.Need to perform settings in Asset accounting

  Asset Accounting Configuration relating to Fiscal year change

   Step1: Define Reduction rules for shortened Fiscal Year

   Path: SPRO->Financial Accounting->Asset Accounting->Valuation->Fiscal Year->Shortened Fiscal Years->Define Reduction Rules for Shortened Fiscal Years

   Step2: Generate Period Control Rules

   Path: Financial Accounting->Asset Accounting ->Depreciation->Valuation Methods->Period Control->Generate Period Controls OA84

  Step3: No reduction is Short year for depreciation key

  Path: SPRO->Financial Accounting->Asset Accounting->Valuation->Depreciation->Valuation Methods->Depreciation Key->Maintain Depreciation Key AFAMA

4.Need to perform period end process again FI & CO

Difficulties :

Dump / run time errors in the settlement of orders / asset accounting

These issues resolved by applying the SAP OSS ( Support) notes

Current FY ====> Calender Year as below

Now need to shift from Jan to dec to April to march from year 2021 onwards

     FY conversions to be defined as a Year dependent FY

     Base year to be defined as Shorted FY

     FY 2021====> Jan 2021 till March 2021====> No of periods==>3Periods

Save 

FY 2020

FY2020===> Jan 2020 to Dec 2020

Save

Do not save then click on Fiscal year Variant

Maintain for year 2022

Do not save then click on Fiscal Year variant

Save

entry.title: Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release
entry.content:
The second major release of the year 2024 is here! The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release is filled with innovations that boost your productivity and support your everyday work life.

I am thrilled to give you further insights on our fully-fledged cloud ERP, supporting your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise in this blog post.

If you prefer rather watching than reading, you are welcome to check out my product update video:

Figure 1: Check out my top highlights of the release in this video.

Before I start with feature-based innovations, I am proud to announce that we have generally uplifted our system availability service level agreement for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Integrated Business Planning from 99.7% to 99.9% for all our customers. This leap signals our reliability and the investments into technology, hardware, procedures, and automation that we continuously make to deliver a world-class SaaS ERP service to you. This marks another superior level of service availability and helps customers to plan business operations with greater precision at less risks. To put it into context: This cuts the upgrade downtimes by another 66%.

The new service level agreement sets a high standard for our cloud operations moving forward. 

And there’s more: Since mid-July, GROW with SAP premium subscription has expanded to include SAP Sales Cloud and Concur Expense solutions. Both enhances our Cloud ERP and gives it more horizontal width to allow customers bring more use cases onboard our solution.

And we keep the good news coming! While delivering more functional and technical based value to our public cloud customers, we reduce the complexity of monetizing digital business models by bringing contract accounting and invoicing capabilities into the core SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offering. With the 2408 release, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for contract and accounting will be part of GROW with SAP. We are giving our customers full access to the high volume, high performance and high automation invoicing, and subledger solution that focuses on service, subscriptions, pay-per-use, and multi-sided business models, without extra metering based on revenue and expenses. No action required for our existing GROW customers, the expanded functionality is included up to their licensed GROW FUEs and will automatically become available from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 for all customers.

Learn more about GROW here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/grow.html


Figure 2: Discover the value of GROW with SAP.

We offer you even more room to GROW: Constantly updating our methodology stays important! For this release, we are excited to announce significant updates to our GROW Adoption Plan, designed to streamline and enhance the adoption process for customers and partners. By integrating new deliverables and accelerators into the GROW Adoption Plan and SAP Cloud ALM, companies can achieve a faster and more efficient implementation experience. This release features GROW Adoption Plan tagging, combining new adoption deliverables and related accelerators, including the Multi-Country Deployment and Application Lifecycle Management accelerators with new content. Additionally, our updated quality gates now include the first iteration of adoption elements, which will further integrate into SAP Cloud ALM.

Available for some time, but in between releases and therefore, potentially new to some, we are also offering our customers guidance for transitioning from the two-system-based landscape to the three-system landscape of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.


Figure 3: We are offering our customers guidance for transitioning from the two-system-based landscape to the three-system landscape of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

Also, SAP introduces new sustainability capabilities to support organizations in meeting their sustainability ambitions and goals as well as regulatory requirements. These include automated ESG reporting via the SAP Sustainability Control Tower and AI-enhanced carbon footprint calculations through the SAP Sustainability Footprint Management solution. Additionally, both solutions feature a carbon calculator engine for comprehensive emissions reporting, and the upcoming SAP Green Ledger will integrate greenhouse gas emissions into financial decision-making.

 

Speaking of artificial intelligence: A lot has happened here in the past months. With SAP’s Business AI strategy, we are defining the future by embedding SAP Business AI along our entire portfolio:

Relevant – Reliable – Responsible

Figure 4: With SAP Business AI, we accelerate your business processes and optimize decision-making.

Enough upfront news–without any further ado, let’s check out what I additionally consider as highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release.

 

Modern and Intelligent Experience

SAP User Experience

Through artificial intelligence, we help to get your work done faster and change the user experience–starting with our generative AI copilot, Joule. After a successful phased roll-out in the Early Adopter Care program, Joule will grace your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system with a continuous delivery shipment very soon, at current state of planning in quarter 3/2024. Joule works as follows: Formulate your business need in natural language and Joule will provide easy access to applications and data, lowering manual tasks and boosting productivity.

Figure 5: Our natural-language, generative AI copilot Joule provides easy access to applications and data.

 

SAP Business AI offers you a cutting-edge SAP Fiori experience via smart summarization and easy filter. Smart summarization jump starts your collaboration or follow-up by extracting the essential information of the object pages of the SAP Fiori elements applications you are using in the system. With easy filter, you can select data of “list report-based" SAP Fiori apps instead of defining filter values manually. All of this in natural language. These AI use-cases are available in beta testing. Check out the SAP Customer Influence page to sign up: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3698

Figure 6: AI-assisted smart summarization extracts the essential information of the object pages of the SAP Fiori elements apps you’re using in the system.

Figure 7: With easy filter, you can select data of “list report-based" SAP Fiori apps instead of defining filter values manually.

 

To enhance transparency around significant system changes, we’ve established a scalable communication strategy, allowing the delivery of important messages to specifically chosen customers and users, following the "need-to-know" principle. This in-app communication was highly requested by our customers and partners through advisory groups and our influence portal and permits us to spotlight essential customer activities in the system. Catering messages to such specific requirements facilitates delivering reminder information solely to those customers who haven't yet performed important requisite system activities and those users who possess the necessary authorization to carry out these tasks. A first example of this approach is the targeted communication pertaining to SAP Fiori launchpad settings, i.e., the switch to spaces and pages.

Figure 8: Target users get system pop-ups after login showing critical must-dos and must-knows for their user role including a linked document for details.

 

Finance

In finance, we introduce new review booklet for cost centers. This app provides a concise display of both actual and planned costs, organizing several predefined reports under 'Actuals' and 'Plan/Actuals' pages. It provides business applications that embed crucial insights into the relevant business context, supporting the answering of critical business queries effectively and efficiently. It seamlessly integrates application logic, guidance, and multidimensional insights and upholds the promise of "insight to action" within embedded analytics, enabling users to analyze actuals and plan data in a single report while providing the flexibility to toggle between guided views and generic slicing, dicing, and pivoting based on specific business requirements.

Figure 9: Display, analyze, and validate cost center data with the new review booklet.

Additionally, we enable you to analyze and summarize data from the cost-center review booklet into actionable financial business insights with the help of generative AI. This use case is available in beta testing. Check out the SAP Customer Influence page to sign up: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3698

Figure 10: Gain financial business insights with the help of generative AI.

 

GRC, Trade, and Tax

This release delivers an innovative experience in managing US-internal tax configuration powered by Joule. The process of maintaining tax jurisdiction codes and rates has been simplified from a dozen steps to only three! Users just can use a natural language prompt to retrieve detailed information from the AI, then verify, simulate, and save the configured tax jurisdiction codes and rates. Once the connection between your SAP system and the LLM is configured, users can chat directly with the AI to retrieve detailed address-based tax jurisdiction information. Furthermore, the LLM delivers links to the respective state tax or revenue authority, providing users with an easily accessible way to verify their tax rate information prior to simulating and saving the changes. This use case is currently available in beta testing. To register, check out the SAP Customer Influence page: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3698

Figure 11: Joule helps to simplify the process of maintaining US-internal tax configuration.

 

Sales

One of the key highlights here is the auto-activation of the intelligent scenario for input recommendations for sales orders. This feature significantly boosts efficiency and cost savings by hastening the activation of the intelligent scenario for sales order recommendations. Implemented when scope item 73P gets activated, the system employs an auto-training mechanism that periodically trains the machine learning model based on the business data from the past year. Users have the flexibility to enable or disable this auto-training in the ‘Intelligent Scenario Management’ app. This functionality guarantees automatic set-up, training and operation of the intelligent scenario, making the sales order completion more efficient and intuitive while reducing costs.

Figure 12: Auto-activation of the intelligent scenario for input recommendations makes the sales-orders process more efficient and intuitive.

 

Product Compliance

With this release, we enable the generation of product compliance documents with integration into the safety data sheet process. Users can now generate a first selection of sections of an EU safety data sheet (without country/region-specific additions) for product compliance solutions using Adobe Document Services. This not only extends the product compliance solution with integrated document generation capability but also streamlines the generation of documents for product compliance data. This is based on versatile Adobe form templates and form data providers. The generated documents are seamlessly integrated into the safety data sheet process. This also comes with new apps to manage and release data for handling and storage as well as stability and reactivity of products, making it a comprehensive tool for product compliance.

Figure 13: You can now easily generate a first selection of sections of an EU safety data sheet using Adobe Document Services.

 

ERP Innovations

Manufacturing

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 introduces advancements in the ‘Monitor Material Coverage’ SAP Fiori apps. The ‘Monitor Material Coverage – Net Segments’ and the ‘Monitor Material Coverage – Net and Individual Segments’ apps now offer additional columns displaying the number of rescheduling proposals for a material. This improvement assists businesses in avoiding supply shortages or an excess of supplies. This is achieved by indicating the number of Material Requirements Planning (MRP) supplying elements with rescheduling proposals for each material. Additionally, the system can reschedule in, reschedule out, or cancel a process based upon a calculation for the entire planning horizon. The enhanced features enable better management of supply shortages and excess supply with MRP material coverage apps, providing a more effective and streamlined operation.

Figure 14: Manage supply shortages or excess supply with additional columns in the ‘Monitor Material Coverage’ SAP Fiori apps.

 

Supply Chain Management

In this release, we ship the warehouse-driven split of the outbound delivery order. This feature empowers organizations to separate packed and unpacked items, quantities, or handling units from an existing outbound delivery order into a new one. This offers increased flexibility in case a complete picking is not possible, added functionality if space in the truck is limited, and the ability to split outbound delivery order items for which no warehouse task has been created yet. It’s also possible to split handling units into a new outbound delivery order. The release therefore provides businesses with improved logistical abilities, offering greater adaptability in managing outbound delivery orders.

Figure 15: The warehouse-driven split of the outbound delivery order empower organizations to separate packed and unpacked items, quantities, or handling units from an existing outbound delivery order into a new one.

 

Solution and Subscription Business

Here, I want to highlight a feature from the latest CFD release which I believe has huge business value for organizations transitioning to solution-led business models. The introduction of advanced solution bundling enables you to pre-configure complex solution orders which can contain multiple configurable and non-configurable items, and sell them on a single solution order, providing greater flexibility whilst saving effort on manual input. This enables the sale of complex end-to-end solution offerings tailored to specific customer needs.

Figure 16: Pre-configured bundles can be added to a solution order and processed for further fulfilment.

 

Service

In this release, we enable you to add maintenance plan templates in service contracts. This enhancement simplifies the workflow drastically, particularly for those dealing with service contracts that include maintenance plans. During the creation process of service contract templates, you can already add maintenance plan templates to service contract items. After creating a service contract from a service contract template, the maintenance plan templates are also copied. When you release a service contract item, a new maintenance plan is automatically created using the maintenance plan template. If you don’t use service contract templates, you can add maintenance plan templates directly in a service contract.

Figure 17: Maintenance plan templates facilitate the service contract process significantly.

 

Enterprise Porfolio and Project Management

To better support the engineer-to-order process, we ship an innovation to reserve materials using project demand. When it comes to project demands, we offer three categories: resources, services, and materials. In the category of materials, we now offer ‘Project Stock Reservation’. A project procurement controller or project manager can select the WBS element, the plant, the material ID, the quantity, and eventually also the storage location, and then reserve material for a project on a certain date. Once the demand is requested, a reservation number gets generated. With the monitoring capabilities, you can view how many quantities have been delivered in case certain quantities in stock are issued against the material. This creates transparency for the project procurement controller.

Figure 18: The ability to reserve material using project demand makes workflows more efficient and project management more effective.

 

Professional Services

The shared risk-based revenue recognition for professional service projects feature addresses potential customer projects that exceed the initial scope, allowing service providers to bill at regular rates before charging reduced rates for any additional work. A new revenue recognition key (SPTMSR) supports this shared risk model. This enables more precise revenue recognition where additional work is required, increasing flexibility for revenue recognition and reducing manual efforts to adjust accrued revenues. In this model, if the need for additional work is identified, the system considers the reduced rate during revenue recognition. The feature signifies an important step towards enhancing accounting accuracy and transparency in service-oriented sectors, promoting mitigation of financial risk, and improving overall project management.

Figure 19: Shared risk-based revenue recognition for professional service projects enhances accuracy and transparency.

 

Sourcing and Procurement

A purchase contract is an agreement between a buyer and a supplier with agreed pricing, quantity, terms, and conditions. Since purchase contracts are long-term documents, they are subject to change over time. By enabling versioning of purchase contracts, businesses can meticulously track various versions of contracts each time a modification is made to an active purchase contract. Following alterations to fields set for versioning, the system automatically creates a new version. The new version can then become an active document, subject to the approval process. The versioning feature enhances transparency and efficiency by enabling comparisons between two document versions, including changes notable in pricing. This function ultimately aids in fostering improved collaboration between buyers and suppliers.

Figure 20: Versioning in purchase contracts fosters improved collaboration between buyers and suppliers.

 

Asset Management

In this release, we introduce an enhanced feature of recording the "As-Found" condition in the 'Perform Maintenance Jobs' app. The app offers maintenance technicians an improved way to record the initial condition of an asset during proactive and routine maintenance jobs, even when the maintenance order does not have a maintenance notification assigned. If the specific technical object differs from the overall order header, a new notification will be created automatically while the technician records the "As-Found" condition. Previously, the malfunction data tab was enabled only if the order has an associated notification. Now, these tabs are accessible by default in orders with notifications. Additionally, configurations can allow users to maintain malfunction data, thereby providing users with better recording of asset reliability data which in turn enhances the overall user experience.

Figure 21: We enhance the recording of the “As-Found” condition in the ‘Perform Maintenance Jobs’ app.

 

Environment, Health, and Safety

A new application for managing the investigation process significantly improves the tracking and oversight of investigation projects, allowing for more effective and efficient processes. The ‘Manage Investigations’ app offers a comprehensive platform where pertinent details such as investigation phase, persons involved, and timelines can be meticulously documented and managed. Administrators can determine the course and progression of investigations, and delegate necessary tasks for seamless operation. This new tool pursues both procedural integrity and simplicity, with a user-friendly interface coupled with robust functionality. Ultimately, it enhances user experience, strengthens accountability, and fosters greater efficiency in managing the investigation process.

Figure 22: The new ‘Manage Investigations’ app fosters great efficiency in managing the investigation process.

 

Two-Tier ERP

In two-tier ERP, Tier 1 acts as an original equipment manufacturer on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and Tier 2 acts as a service subsidiary on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.  With this set up, organizations can perform claims processing and subsequent reimbursements between their service organization and original equipment manufacturer. In this release, we introduce a refined warranty claim process, aiding you in effectively managing and navigating through the complexities of warranty claims. The process offers improved visibility into various aspects such as claim creation, processing, submission, and tracking. In addition, the solution accelerates assessment and administration of warranty claims with built-in automation that streamlines the end-to-end warranty management. As a part of service management, this efficient mechanism ensures quicker resolution time, reducing both manual effort and the overall operating cost for the business. Essentially, the enhancement in the warranty claim process signifies an evolution towards improved customer satisfaction and a more strategic approach to service operations.

Figure 23: The new warranty claim process offers improved customer satisfaction and a more strategic approach to service operations.

 

SaaS Foundation

Extensibility

The cloud extensibility model with a three-system landscape in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition enables customers to seamlessly leverage SAP innovations, resulting in future-proof extensions that are compliant throughout their lifecycle and do not require adjustments during cloud innovation cycles. With this model, we can develop cloud-ready business apps, services, or custom extensions that are both upgrade-stable and cloud-ready. With developer extensibility, as a customer or partner, you can use one-off delivery for ABAP development intended to remain within your SAP S/4HANA Cloud landscape which enables you to import the developed custom ABAP code in a three-system landscape into the test and production system of the current landscape.

With multi-off delivery, we take it a step further. Custom development can be performed by a partner on behalf of a customer, in a separate development system which is not connected to the customer landscape, and then deployed on the customer's three-system landscape using the ‘Git-Enabled Change and Transport System’ (gCTS). The source code of ABAP extensions is stored in remote Git repositories, with gCTS being used to transfer (push and pull) the ABAP custom code between SAP S/4HANA Cloud landscapes and Git repositories. This is especially useful for scenarios where source and target landscapes can't be connected via transport routes. The SAP Fiori app ‘Git-Enabled CTS (gCTS)’ helps to manage tasks such as connecting to remote Git repositories, pushing and pulling commits, and managing branches. Commits are created in the development environment using ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) when a task of a transport request is released. Find more information here.

 

Figures 24 & 25: Multi-off delivery brings a multitude of possibilities for custom development.

 

Public Sector Management

Public sector functionality within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is broadening in terms of geographical availability. In addition to the US, Canada, and Germany, services such as grants management, budget management, and fund accounting is now also available in the UK, the Netherlands, and Australia. The global deployment of our service has started and is gaining great momentum. Be prepared for the availability in additional countries by the year 2025.

Additionally, we bring new, comprehensive budget management functionalities:

  • Addition of a Substitution Tool for Budget Maintenance.
  • Enhancement allowing the consumption of budget for expected expenses rather than the posting date.
  • Feature to check Public Sector budget availability using project hierarchies.
  • Updates to facilitate the generation of budget documents.
  • Improved reporting on budgets versus payments and open invoices.

Figure 26: This release includes significant updates to the early release enablement cycle for public sector.

 

R&D Engineering

In this release, we ship the integration of CAD design for data and file management. With this SAP Product Lifecycle Management solution, seamless interaction between engineering designs and SAP business processes is now feasible, i.e., you can easily transfer engineering bills of material (EBOMs) from the SAP Product Lifecycle Management solution and continue to work on them as manufacturing bills of material (MBOMs) in the relevant ‘Maintain Bill of Material (Version 2)’ app. The CAD tools extend their capabilities by being able to store files in the SAP PLM Product Data Integration (PDI), leading to the development of an Enterprise Product Structure within SAP’s Product Lifecycle Management. This integration simplifies the transfer of product data via product handover to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, reducing the number of interfaces and facilitating a smoother operation. The end result is higher efficiency in engineering processes, holistic product description for increased transparency, and reduced operating costs. Overall, it signifies a major stride towards optimizing product data management in industry-specific CAD applications.

Figure 27: The integration of CAD design for data and file management enhances your engineering processes.

 

Business Partner Data

This release presents an enhancement in data privacy management, allowing users to concurrently assign data controllers along with specific purposes to business partners. This upgrade significantly bolsters the concept of “privacy by design and default” by permitting users the flexibility to manually or automatically attach data controllers to business partners. With options to leverage this feature via the ‘Maintain Business Partner’ and ‘Manage Business Partner’ SAP Fiori apps or through API channels, businesses can effectively manage their partner master data. This advancement not only ensures timely compliance with stringent data protection regulations but also molds business processes to instill trust among partners and customers. By enhancing the data privacy landscape, this feature equips businesses with the edge to meet legal requirements and foster a venerable relationship with their customers.

Figure 28: You can now assign data controllers along with specific purposes to business partners.

 

Information Lifecycle Management

SAP Information Lifecycle Management (SAP ILM) allows users to manage the lifecycle of live and archived data. Users can set rules for managing data throughout its lifecycle, specifying how long it should stay in the database, how long it should be archived, and when it should be deleted. This can be done consistently across development, test, and production systems. With the 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, one key feature is the self-service configuration user interface (SSCUI). This tool allows users to centrally maintain SAP ILM settings and transport them across the system landscape i.e., development, test and production systems, reducing the risk of errors and inconsistencies. Another key feature with this release is the storage of archive files to SAP HANA Cloud data lake files, ensuring fast and efficient access to archived data. Previously, these files were stored in the Neo platform. This change helps to store the SAP ILM archive files on a future-proof, fast, and cost-efficient technology stack, supporting both SAP ILM use cases: data volume management and data privacy. This change exclusively affects the underlying technology stack, with no impact on usability.

You can find more information here.

 

Figures 29 & 30: SAP Information Lifecycle Management (SAP ILM) allows users to manage the lifecycle of live and archived data.

 

Identity and Access Management

Unlock the full potential of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the unified access to documentation about Identity and Access Management (IAM) on SAP Help Portal! With this, we provide a simplified, one-stop shop for all IAM-related documentation, making it easier for users to access everything you need to know about user and identity management, roles, authorization, and authentication. The documentation covers all IAM-related tasks in the different phases of implementation (prepare, realize, run, and upgrade) making the process more streamlined and efficient. This unified access to IAM documentation provides a holistic view on IAM for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and integrated products from initial system access to tasks which need to be completed before, during, and after upgrading SAP S/4HANA Cloud and allows you to familiarize yourself with its key concepts. 

Figure 31: Check out the documentation about Identity and Access Management on SAP Help Portal.

 

Summary

You see, the world is turning fast, and we keep you at the forefront of progress–but these are by far not all innovations we have in store with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. Stay tuned for the individual blog posts diving deeper into selected lines of business and industries. 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn to not miss the next update.

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

🔎Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Collective Billing of Solution Order:Include Prof.Serv. Project and Other Docs into single invoice
entry.content:
Co-authored by Klaus Kistner

Solution Business Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enables you to sell bundled solutions combining products, services, subscriptions, long-term services, and projects to become a one-stopshop to solve complete customer problems [Ref : Selling Business Outcomes with Bundled Solutions].

As part of this scenario, it can make sense to create a single invoice per solution order.

In previous releases, it was not possible to converge billing data from customer projects (that are part of the same solution order) into a combined invoice with billing data from other documents that are also part of the solution order, such as service orders, service contract, and so on. [Ref: One Invoice Per Solution Order]. Customer projects had to be invoiced separately.

As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408, you can use billing type CBD2 to combine all billing-relevant follow-on documents of a solution order into a single invoice. Refer to note 3413050 for an example of how to create combined invoice of the solution order document.
